_meta.dna: windows(native) = msi(signed) x defender(trusted) x registry(clean) x hidpi(scaled) x narrator(accessible) x enterprise(gpo) x wsl(compatible)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. During execution, expand to ~100 questions per persona. Windows is the largest desktop market. Enterprise IT admins, gamers, and office workers all expect a polished Windows experience. Every question must probe whether Solace Browser is a first-class Windows citizen.

name: Tower of Windows
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Is the MSI installer signed with a valid code signing certificate?
Does the installer work for both per-user and per-machine installation?
Can IT admins deploy the MSI silently via Group Policy or SCCM?
Does the installer register the app in Add/Remove Programs?
Is the uninstaller thorough (removes all files, registry entries, scheduled tasks)?
Does the installer handle upgrades in-place without data loss?
Is the installer tested on Windows 10 and Windows 11?
Does Windows Defender flag the installer or executable as suspicious?
Does SmartScreen show 'unrecognized app' warnings?
Is the executable signed with an EV code signing certificate for SmartScreen reputation?
Does the app trigger false positives in enterprise antivirus solutions?
Does real-time protection slow down Solace Browser operations?
Can the app be whitelisted in Windows Security without admin intervention?
Does the app's network activity trigger firewall alerts?
Does the app follow Windows 11 Fluent Design guidelines?
Are rounded corners, Mica material, or acrylic blur used appropriately?
Does the app support Windows dark/light mode via prefers-color-scheme?
Does the title bar match the Windows 11 style?
Are standard keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+C/V/X, Alt+F4, etc.) working?
Does the app support Snap Layouts for window management?
Does the app integrate with the Windows 11 notification center?
Does the app render correctly at 100%, 125%, 150%, 175%, and 200% scaling?
Are all icons and images sharp at high DPI settings?
Does the app handle per-monitor DPI scaling on multi-monitor setups?
Does the app respond to DPI changes when moved between monitors?
Are there any blurry text or images at non-100% scaling?
Does the app handle Windows display scaling changes without restart?
Are dialog boxes and popups correctly positioned at all scaling levels?
Can a Narrator user navigate all UI elements of Solace Browser?
Are ARIA labels correctly announced by Narrator?
Does the YinYang approval dialog work with Narrator?
Can Narrator users access the settings page and change themes?
Are focus indicators visible in Windows High Contrast mode?
Does Narrator's scan mode work for reading page content?
Are keyboard shortcuts discoverable through Narrator?
Does the app register URL protocol handlers correctly?
Are registry entries created under the correct hive (HKCU for per-user)?
Does the uninstaller remove all registry entries?
Are file associations registered properly?
Does the app use the registry for auto-start configuration?
Are registry entries compatible with registry-based GPO management?
Does the app handle registry access denial gracefully?
Does the OAuth3 vault integrate with Windows Credential Manager?
Are credentials stored with appropriate persistence and protection levels?
Can users see Solace entries in Windows Credential Manager?
Are credentials cleaned up on app uninstall?
Does the vault fallback to encrypted file storage if Credential Manager is unavailable?
Does Windows Hello (biometric) unlock work for the vault?
Are credential storage operations atomic and crash-safe?

floors.persona_domain: taskbar, system tray, and jump list
questions: Does the app pin correctly to the Windows taskbar?
Does the taskbar icon show a badge for pending approvals?
Is there a system tray icon for background operation?
Does right-clicking the taskbar icon show a useful Jump List?
Does the app support taskbar progress indication for long operations?
Can the daemon mode run as a system tray-only application?
Does the app handle taskbar icon scaling at different DPI levels?
Does the app send notifications through the Windows notification system?
Are notifications actionable (approve/reject buttons in the toast)?
Do notifications appear in Action Center for later review?
Can users configure notification settings in Windows Settings?
Do notifications respect Focus Assist / Do Not Disturb?
Are notification sounds appropriate and configurable?
Does the app handle notification interaction correctly?

floors.persona_domain: background service and scheduled task management
questions: Can the daemon mode run as a Windows Service?
Is the service registered correctly in services.msc?
Does the service start automatically on boot?
Can the service be managed via sc.exe or PowerShell?
Is the service resilient to crashes (auto-restart on failure)?
Does the service run under the correct user account?
Can the service communicate with the GUI app?
Can solace-cli be invoked from PowerShell and cmd.exe?
Is the PATH configured correctly during installation?
Does the CLI handle Windows file paths (backslash, spaces, UNC)?
Can PowerShell scripts automate Solace Browser operations?
Is there tab completion for PowerShell (Register-ArgumentCompleter)?
Does the CLI handle Windows line endings (CRLF) correctly?
Can the CLI output be piped to other Windows commands?
Can IT admins configure Solace Browser via Group Policy?
Is there an ADMX template for GPO-based configuration?
Can GPO disable specific features (custom themes, store access)?
Does the app respect proxy settings configured via GPO?
Can IT admins force-install extensions or apps via GPO?
Is the app compatible with Microsoft Intune management?
Can IT admins lock down the OAuth3 vault configuration?
Does the app create Windows Firewall rules during installation?
Are firewall rules scoped to necessary ports only?
Does the app work behind corporate firewalls with deep packet inspection?
Can the cloudflared tunnel work through Windows Firewall?
Does the app handle proxy authentication (NTLM, Kerberos)?
Are firewall rules cleaned up on uninstall?
Does the app prompt for firewall exceptions or handle denial gracefully?
Does the app handle long file paths (>260 characters) on Windows?
Are file permissions set correctly (not world-readable for vault data)?
Does the app handle NTFS alternate data streams?
Are file names safe (no reserved characters or names like CON, PRN)?
Does the app handle file locking correctly on Windows?
Are temporary files stored in %TEMP% or the app's data directory?
Does the app handle OneDrive Files On-Demand (cloud-only files)?
Does the app survive Windows Updates without breaking?
Is the app compatible with Windows Insider Preview builds?
Does the app handle .NET Framework version changes?
Are Visual C++ redistributables bundled or handled correctly?
Does the app work on Windows 10 LTSC (enterprise long-term)?
Is there a compatibility testing matrix for Windows versions?
Does the app handle Windows version detection correctly?
Can solace-cli installed in WSL interact with the Windows GUI browser?
Does the app handle cross-WSL/Windows file path translation?
Can WSL Python run Solace Browser automation scripts?
Does the app work when installed in both WSL and Windows?
Can WSL users access the Windows vault and evidence store?
Does the cloudflared tunnel work from WSL?
Is there documentation for WSL-specific setup?
Does the CLI render colors and formatting correctly in Windows Terminal?
Are Unicode characters (emojis, CJK) displayed correctly?
Does the CLI handle Windows Terminal profiles?
Can users create a Solace Browser profile in Windows Terminal?
Does the CLI support Windows Terminal's tab and pane features?
Are ANSI escape codes used correctly for Windows Terminal?
Does the CLI work in ConEmu, Cmder, and other terminal emulators?
